item.title: Takeaways from Ken Paxton's Texas runoff win over John Cornyn
item.description: Tuesday's runoff election in Texas saw another Republican incumbent lose his seat to a Trump-backed nominee. Four-term Sen. John Cornyn conceded the race to state Attorney General Ken Paxton, who picked up a late endorsement from President Trump. CBS News senior White House and political correspondent Ed O'Keefe has more from the Lone Star State.

item.title: Trump says Iran is "negotiating on fumes" and that he doesn't care about midterms
item.description: President Trump held a Cabinet meeting at the White House on Wednesday where he said talks with the Iranian regime are ongoing, but left the door open to resume fighting if necessary. CBS News' Natalie Brand and Charlie D'Agata have more.

item.title: What to make of Trump administration's latest comments on Iran
item.description: White House officials spoke about the war in Iran during a Cabinet meeting with President Trump on Wednesday. CBS News national security contributor Joseph Zacks has more.

item.title: Top FEMA official Bob Fenton says "we're ready for hurricane season"
item.description: FEMA says it's ready for hurricane season, though it's still racing to recover from months of shutdown disruptions, delayed grants and a depleted Disaster Relief Fund.

item.title: Trump visited soldiers at Walter Reed — but not the 14 injured in the Iran war
item.description: More than a dozen soldiers injured in Operation Epic Fury are still recovering at the military hospital.

item.title: James Talarico on past comments about God, Senate race with Ken Paxton, more
item.description: Democrat James Talarico will face off with Republican Ken Paxton for a U.S. Senate seat after Paxton beat Sen. John Cornyn in Texas' GOP primary runoff Wednesday night. Talarico spoke with Ed O'Keefe about his campaign.

item.title: Takeaways from Texas' primaries: Trump's power, Latino vote, redistricting and more
item.description: The November ballot in Texas is now set, after a four-term Republican senator lost to a Trump-endorsed challenger in Tuesday's primary runoffs. Meanwhile, Texas Democrats are angling to win their first Senate race since 1988.

item.title: 2 dead, 9 workers "unrecovered" after Washington chemical tank implosion
item.description: The damaged tank at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, a chemical used in paper processing, authorities said.

item.title: Trump paints murky picture of Iran peace talks in comments at Cabinet meeting
item.description: President Trump spoke at length about the Iran war during a Cabinet meeting at the White House Wednesday, but his comments left many questions about the current state of peace negotiations. CBS News chief White House correspondent Nancy Cordes reports.

item.title: "The Takeout" celebrates 1 year on the air
item.description: "The Takeout" premiered on CBS News 24/7 exactly one year ago. Major Garrett marks the milestone.

item.title: YouTube taking steps to make clear when realistic videos are made by AI
item.description: YouTube said it will automatically label photorealistic content created by AI, the video platform said.

item.title: YouTube to automatically label AI-generated content
item.description: YouTube unveiled a new AI labeling system on Wednesday, aiming to make it easier for viewers to distinguish between real and AI content. CNET editor-at-large Scott Stein joins CBS News to break down the change.

item.title: Stocks are rallying despite the Iran war and stubborn inflation. Here's why.
item.description: The S&P 500 hit a new record on Wednesday, even as soaring gas prices fuel inflation and consumer confidence sinks.

item.title: GPS data appears to contradict Brian Hooker's account of wife's disappearance
item.description: After forensic evidence appeared to contradict Brian Hooker's account of where Lynette Hooker was​ the night she disappeared, U.S. investigators are renewing their search in the Bahamas.

item.title: Justice Dept. to start fast-tracking benefits fraud enforcement
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice tells CBS News it will speed up review of certain whistleblower complaints dealing with fraud against benefits programs like Medicare.

item.title: Biden sues Justice Department to block release of files from biographer interviews
item.description: Former President Joe Biden has sued the Justice Department seeking to block the release of files related to interviews he conducted with a biographer that later became a central part of a special counsel investigation into his handling of classified documents.

item.title: Here's how much interest a $90,000 CD account can earn savers now
item.description: A $90,000 CD account may feel unconventional, but the interest-earning ability is still worth knowing for savers.

item.title: Conservative tech critic seen as leading candidate to be DOJ antitrust chief
item.description: The former assistant attorney general for the Justice Department Antitrust Division, Gail Slater, was abruptly terminated in February after clashes with Pam Bondi and Todd Blanche.

item.title: Official complains of persecution over CIA agents' deaths in Mexico
item.description: Two CIA agents died in a car crash while accompanying soldiers and state prosecutors in a raid of a drug lab in Chihuahua.

item.title: Jill Biden: "I was frightened" by 2024 debate, thought husband was having a stroke
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said she thought her husband, Joe Biden, was having a stroke during the 2024 debate against Donald Trump.

item.title: Jill Biden: "I was frightened" by Biden's debate performance and thought he was having a stroke
item.description: Former first lady Jill Biden said she was "frightened" by her husband Joe Biden's performance at the 2024 presidential debate. "As I watched it, I thought, 'Oh, my God, he's having a stroke.' And it scared me to death," Biden told Sunday Morning's Rita Braver in an interview airing Sunday.

item.title: Matthew Perry's assistant who injected him with fatal dose of ketamine gets over 3 years in prison
item.description: Kenneth Iwamasa, 60, is the fifth and final person sentenced for playing a role in Matthew Perry's 2023 ketamine death.

item.title: Iran "negotiating on fumes," Trump says, as Israel ramps up war in Lebanon
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran keep up the bluster but appear ready to de-escalate, Israel expands deadly attacks on the complex war's other front in Lebanon.

item.title: How much money you need to earn to afford a home in 49 U.S. cities
item.description: A household earning the average income would need to spend 40% of its income to afford the typical U.S. home, according to Redfin.

item.title: Trump holds Cabinet meeting as negotiations continue to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump held a Cabinet meeting at the White House after moving it from Camp David due to weather.

item.title: Full interview: Democratic Texas Senate nominee James Talarico
item.description: State Rep. James Talarico, the Democratic candidate for Texas Senate, speaks with CBS News' Ed O'Keefe for his first network interview since state Attorney General Ken Paxton defeated incumbent John Cornyn in the GOP runoff election. Talarico called Paxton the "most corrupt politician in America" and slammed him for giving out "Epstein-style sweetheart deals to pedophiles."

item.title: TV bailiff accused of murder sobs, "I live with it every day"
item.description: Renard Spivey was found not guilty of his wife's murder, but he says he still can't sleep at night.

item.title: James Talarico responds to GOP attacks over past remark that God is "nonbinary"
item.description: Texas state Rep. and Democratic Senate nominee James Talarico responded to Republican attacks over his past remark that God is "nonbinary." "There are some statements that I've made that I certainly regret," He told CBS News' Ed O'Keefe, but accused his GOP opponent Ken Paxton of "intentionally clipping my cringey comments to distract from his career of corruption."

item.title: Fire at kosher store in London "non-suspicious," police say
item.description: London police say a fire at a kosher store in a London neighborhood with a large Jewish community that's been targeted repeatedly is "non-suspicious."

item.title: Stocks mixed as investors shrug off renewed fighting in Iran
item.description: Oil prices were also mixed after U.S. strikes on Iranian forces, underscoring the risks still hanging over markets and consumers.
